verification_notes: 18-hole par 72 confirmed by multiple sources (1golf.eu, allsquaregolf.com, golflux.com, golfpass.com). Yardage varies by source and tee: GolfLux lists 6,756 yards (blue tees); AllSquare lists 6,185 yards (black), 5,794 (blue); 1golf.eu shows 6,759. Using 6,756 from GolfLux (blue tee, likely standard visitor benchmark). Designer: GolfLux attributes to Sukitti Klangvisa; AllSquare lists as n/a. Year opened: GolfLux states 2004; AllSquare states 2011. Dispute flagged. Address confirmed: 156 Moo 19, Pak Chong, Nakhon Ratchasima 30130. Website: seoulsiamresort.com (confirmed from web search). Phone: +66 44 319 052 per 1golf.eu; also +66 89 945 6262 per web search. Green fees: 1golf.eu lists 400 THB weekday / 800 THB weekend — unusually low, likely green fee only, excluding caddie and cart which are additional. Caddie and cart available; mandatory status unclear. Driving range: not confirmed — likely has putting green (confirmed) but driving range unconfirmed. GPS is model estimate for Pak Chong area near 156 Moo 19. priceRange note for Stage 5: ฿ to ฿฿ (green fee alone 400–800 THB; with caddie+cart total may reach 1,200–1,800 THB).
disputes: Year opened: GolfLux states 2004; AllSquareGolf states 2011. Significant discrepancy — cannot resolve from available sources. Set to 2004 (GolfLux) as provisional; mark for verification.

Green fee: 1golf.eu lists 400 THB weekday / 800 THB weekend. These appear to be green fee only, not inclusive of caddie or cart. Total cost with caddie and cart likely significantly higher. Flagged for direct confirmation.
Caddie/cart requirement: Not confirmed as mandatory from available sources. Listed as unknown (null) for caddie_required and cart_required.

prose.overview: Seoul Siam Resort & Country Club — also known as Pakchong Highland Country Club — is an 18-hole par-72 course set in the mountain terrain of Pak Chong, Nakhon Ratchasima. The course is Korean-affiliated, reflecting the substantial Korean golf tourism market in the Khao Yai region, and its facilities cater specifically to that demographic: the clubhouse includes a Korean restaurant, and the course layout was designed with the sensibilities of Korean golfers in mind. It is a relatively recent addition to the Khao Yai golf scene, built in the early 2000s with a design attributed to Sukitti Klangvisa. The resort facilities include swimming pools, a sauna, fitness centre, and putting green, making it a complete short-break destination. The course is positioned as an accessible option with some of the lowest green fees in the area, appealing to golfers who want to stretch a Khao Yai trip over multiple rounds without significant cost per round.
prose.layout_and_experience: The 18-hole par-72 layout measures approximately 6,756 yards from the blue tees, placing it in the mid-length range. The course design incorporates pristine lakes and varied bunker styles described across multiple sources as rewarding smart course management. GolfLux notes expansive contouring and a layout that punishes wayward drives into the Pak Chong highland terrain. Given the mountain setting, elevation changes add to the visual experience and shot selection challenges. The Korean restaurant and full clubhouse amenities, including sauna and fitness centre, make post-round time at the club genuinely enjoyable. The course is open to the public and is primarily used by a mix of Korean tourists and Thai weekend golfers from Nakhon Ratchasima and Bangkok.
prose.tips: Seoul Siam offers the lowest headline green fees of any Khao Yai area course featured in this guide — the weekday rate is a fraction of premium resort alternatives. Factor in caddie and cart costs to establish your true all-in price before arrival; these are additional to the green fee. The Korean restaurant is a genuine draw — a full Korean barbecue after a round on a cool Pak Chong evening is a memorable combination. The course is best approached as a value-for-money additional round on a multi-day Khao Yai itinerary, particularly for golfers who want to play a different layout each day. Book directly by phone at +66 44 319 052 or via the resort website.
prose.location_and_access: Seoul Siam Resort & Country Club is located at 156 Moo 19, Lamsompung Road, Pak Chong, Nakhon Ratchasima 30130, in the Pak Chong highland area. From central Bangkok, the drive takes approximately two hours via Route 1 (Mittraphap Highway) northbound to Pak Chong, then local roads into the highland resort zone. The distance is approximately 150 km. A private car or resort transfer is required — no practical public transport serves this location.
prose.rental_cta_context: Playing a Khao Yai multi-course itinerary that includes Seoul Siam? LENGOLF delivers TaylorMade and Callaway club sets to your Bangkok hotel before you head north, removing any need to rent locally at each course.
